steps.title: Track the scale changes
steps.content: Cylinder volume is proportional to $r^2h$. Doubling the radius multiplies $r^2$ by $4$, and halving the height multiplies $h$ by $\frac{1}{2}$.

steps.title: Apply the net factor
steps.content: The total volume factor is $$4\cdot\frac{1}{2}=2$$ So the new volume is twice the original volume. $$2(28)=56$$ The answer is C.
